title: 2019年度 第2回検定一次試験 英検準1級 リスニング

parts.name: 第1部
parts.description: 対話を聞き、その質問に対して最も適切な答えを1, 2, 3, 4の中から一つ選ぶ形式です。

lines.speaker: female
lines.text: Hey, Callum. You're in early today.

lines.speaker: male
lines.text: Yeah, my new apartment's much closer to work.

lines.speaker: female
lines.text: How is the new place?

lines.speaker: male
lines.text: Very spacious, but the wallpaper and carpets are horrible. I really need to redecorate.

lines.speaker: female
lines.text: Did you know I was an interior decorator before I joined this advertising agency?

lines.speaker: male
lines.text: Really? You wouldn't consider doing it, would you? I'd pay, of course.

lines.speaker: female
lines.text: Unfortunately, I'm just too busy. But I probably know someone who'd be interested. I'll ask around.

lines.speaker: male
lines.text: Great, thanks.

questions.question: What does the woman offer to do?
choices: Assist Callum in getting a job.
Redecorate Callum's apartment.
Try to find a decorator for Callum.
Help Callum search for a new apartment.
questions.explanation: 女性は自分は忙しくてできないが、興味のある人を知っているかもしれないので探してみると申し出ています。
highlights: I probably know someone who'd be interested. I'll ask around
questions.choiceAnalysis: 選択肢1「就職活動の手伝い」→言及なし。選択肢2「自分で模様替え」→忙しくてできない。選択肢3「装飾担当者を探す」→正解。選択肢4「新しいアパート」→既に引っ越し済み。

lines.speaker: male
lines.text: I'm sure glad that meeting is finally over.

lines.speaker: female
lines.text: Me, too. The other parents didn't seem very friendly, did they?

lines.speaker: male
lines.text: Yeah, I noticed. Well, we did hear that the kind of parents who consider Cumberland High for their children are pretty snobbish. After all, it's an expensive private school.

lines.speaker: female
lines.text: Well, I guess their kids will be pretty much like they are. Plus, it'd be a struggle to keep up with that kind of tuition.

lines.speaker: male
lines.text: We'd get by, but I am worried about the kind of friends our son would have.

lines.speaker: female
lines.text: I totally agree, Jon. Perhaps we should consider a public school after all.

questions.question: What will the couple most likely do?
choices: Consider another school for their son.
Enroll their son in Cumberland.
Get a loan to pay for tuition.
Consult with the other parents.
questions.explanation: 夫婦は他の保護者の態度やそれが子供の友人関係に影響することを心配し、公立学校を検討しようと結論づけています。
highlights: Perhaps we should consider a public school after all
questions.choiceAnalysis: 選択肢1「他の学校を検討」→正解。選択肢2「入学させる」→やめようとしている。選択肢3「ローンを組む」→言及なし。選択肢4「他の保護者に相談」→言及なし。

lines.speaker: female
lines.text: You didn't come to our barbecue yesterday, Wayne. What happened?

lines.speaker: male
lines.text: I went to Nun's Park, but I couldn't find you guys anywhere. I looked around for over an hour.

lines.speaker: female
lines.text: Ah... it was actually at Vivian Park. We couldn't reserve a barbecue pit at Nun's Park. I e-mailed everyone about it.

lines.speaker: male
lines.text: Really? I didn't check my e-mail.

lines.speaker: female
lines.text: Why didn't you call me?

lines.speaker: male
lines.text: Sorry, I forgot to take my phone with me.

lines.speaker: female
lines.text: Well, we'll have another one again soon.

questions.question: Why did Wayne not go to the barbecue yesterday?
choices: He got lost going to the park.
He was not e-mailed about the location.
He had a last-minute appointment.
He went to the wrong place.
questions.explanation: 場所が変更されメールが送られたが、Wayneはメールを確認せず元の公園に行ってしまいました。
highlights: I went to Nun's Park
it was actually at Vivian Park
I didn't check my e-mail
questions.choiceAnalysis: 選択肢1「道に迷った」→場所が違った。選択肢2「メールが来なかった」→メールは送られたが確認しなかった。選択肢3「急用」→言及なし。選択肢4「間違った場所に行った」→正解。

lines.speaker: male
lines.text: Man, that had to be the most stressful job interview I've ever done.

lines.speaker: female
lines.text: What happened? Did it go badly?

lines.speaker: male
lines.text: Not really. But when I walked into the room, there were six guys sitting there.

lines.speaker: female
lines.text: You're kidding! Did they all ask you questions?

lines.speaker: male
lines.text: Only two actually spoke. The others just sat there. They were all taking notes about what I said.

lines.speaker: female
lines.text: That does sound stressful. Were there many other candidates?

lines.speaker: male
lines.text: I've no idea. I didn't see anyone else.

questions.question: Why did the man feel stressed?
choices: He had to talk in front of many people.
He made a big mistake during the interview.
The interviewers asked some difficult questions.
There were a lot of candidates for the job.
questions.explanation: 部屋に入ると6人がいて全員がメモを取っていた。多くの人の前で話さなければならないのがストレスでした。
highlights: there were six guys sitting there
They were all taking notes about what I said
questions.choiceAnalysis: 選択肢1「多くの人の前で話す」→正解。選択肢2「大きなミス」→うまくいった。選択肢3「難しい質問」→言及なし。選択肢4「候補者が多い」→他には会わなかった。

lines.speaker: female
lines.text: Can I help you with something, sir?

lines.speaker: male
lines.text: Yes. I'm looking for a winter coat.

lines.speaker: female
lines.text: Well, this style here is popular this year.

lines.speaker: male
lines.text: I like the design, but not the color. Does it come in dark green?

lines.speaker: female
lines.text: It does, but we only have brown and gray in stock.

lines.speaker: male
lines.text: Well, thanks anyway. I'll try another store.

lines.speaker: female
lines.text: I can order one from our warehouse.

lines.speaker: male
lines.text: How long will it take?

lines.speaker: female
lines.text: About a week.

lines.speaker: male
lines.text: OK. That's fine.

questions.question: What will the man do?
choices: Order the coat he wants.
Keep shopping around.
Take the gray coat.
Try on a different style.
questions.explanation: 男性はダークグリーンのコートを倉庫から注文してもらうことにしました。
highlights: I can order one from our warehouse
OK. That's fine
questions.choiceAnalysis: 選択肢1「欲しいコートを注文する」→正解。選択肢2「他の店で探す」→注文に同意。選択肢3「グレーのコート」→ダークグリーンが欲しい。選択肢4「他のスタイル」→言及なし。

lines.speaker: female
lines.text: You've lost weight, Sergio. Have you been dieting?

lines.speaker: male
lines.text: No. I've just found a new way to exercise.

lines.speaker: female
lines.text: I thought you went jogging regularly?

lines.speaker: male
lines.text: I do. But I always skip it when the weather's bad. Now I've started playing exercise video games.

lines.speaker: female
lines.text: Really? I can't understand how anyone can use those to work out. I really need to be out in the fresh air.

lines.speaker: male
lines.text: I still get outdoors. But when it rains, I can exercise and stay dry!

questions.question: What do we learn about the man?
choices: He does not jog anymore.
He exercises more regularly now.
He has just started a new diet.
He is too busy to work out these days.
questions.explanation: 以前は天気が悪いと運動を休んでいたが、今はビデオゲームでも運動できるので、より定期的に運動しています。
highlights: I always skip it when the weather's bad. Now I've started playing exercise video games
questions.choiceAnalysis: 選択肢1「ジョギングをやめた」→まだしている。選択肢2「より定期的に運動」→正解。選択肢3「ダイエットを始めた」→ダイエットではない。選択肢4「忙しくて運動できない」→運動している。

lines.speaker: female
lines.text: Snider Insurance, Jan Meadows speaking.

lines.speaker: male
lines.text: Hi, Jan. Philip here. How did you manage to get to work with all the snow on the roads?

lines.speaker: female
lines.text: My husband's car has four-wheel drive. It took twice as long as usual, though.

lines.speaker: male
lines.text: Well, my car won't start. I could walk down the hill to the bus, but I'm not sure it'll be running.

lines.speaker: female
lines.text: Maybe you should skip work today. I can handle your calls.

lines.speaker: male
lines.text: Thanks, Jan. I'll do that.

questions.question: What does the man decide to do?
choices: Have his car fixed.
Stay home from work.
Call the bus company.
Get Jan to give him a ride.
questions.explanation: 男性は車が動かず、Janの提案で今日は仕事を休むことにしました。
highlights: Maybe you should skip work today
Thanks, Jan. I'll do that
questions.choiceAnalysis: 選択肢1「車を修理」→言及なし。選択肢2「家にいる」→正解。選択肢3「バス会社に電話」→言及なし。選択肢4「Janに送迎」→言及なし。

lines.speaker: male
lines.text: Hello. How can I help you?

lines.speaker: female
lines.text: I'm moving to Chicago. I want to close my savings account and transfer the funds.

lines.speaker: male
lines.text: Is the new account also with our bank?

lines.speaker: female
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: male
lines.text: In that case, there'll be a small charge. Just fill out this transfer request.

lines.speaker: female
lines.text: Sure. And how long will the transfer take?

lines.speaker: male
lines.text: About 24 hours.

lines.speaker: female
lines.text: Oh, it says I need the bank's street address. I'll need to call to get that.

lines.speaker: male
lines.text: You can use this phone.

lines.speaker: female
lines.text: Thanks.

questions.question: What will the woman do next?
choices: Give the man her current address.
Have the man call her new bank.
Make a deposit at her current bank.
Find out the address of her new bank.
questions.explanation: 女性は新しい銀行の住所が必要なので、電話して確認します。
highlights: I need the bank's street address. I'll need to call to get that
questions.choiceAnalysis: 選択肢1「住所を教える」→銀行の住所が必要。選択肢2「男性に電話させる」→自分で電話。選択肢3「入金する」→言及なし。選択肢4「新しい銀行の住所を調べる」→正解。

lines.speaker: male
lines.text: Olivia, did you get a gift for Cheryl's baby yet?

lines.speaker: female
lines.text: Yes, a nice blue blanket. But now I'm worried it's not such a great idea.

lines.speaker: male
lines.text: It sounds fine to me.

lines.speaker: female
lines.text: What if it's a girl? I can't give a blue blanket to a baby girl.

lines.speaker: male
lines.text: Olivia, it's the twenty-first century. No one worries about those kinds of stereotypes anymore, do they?

lines.speaker: female
lines.text: You'd be surprised how many people still hang on to tradition.

lines.speaker: male
lines.text: In that case, just keep the receipt so Cheryl can exchange it if necessary.

questions.question: Why is Olivia worried?
choices: The gift she bought may be unsuitable.
She should have spent more on the gift.
Cheryl has already received a blanket.
She cannot find the receipt for the blanket.
questions.explanation: もし女の子だったら青いブランケットは不適切かもしれないと心配しています。
highlights: What if it's a girl? I can't give a blue blanket to a baby girl
questions.choiceAnalysis: 選択肢1「贈り物が不適切かも」→正解。選択肢2「もっと高い物を」→言及なし。選択肢3「既に持っている」→言及なし。選択肢4「レシートが見つからない」→男性がレシートを取っておけと言った。

lines.speaker: female
lines.text: Honey, I'm glad I visited my father in the nursing home today. He seemed really excited to see me.

lines.speaker: male
lines.text: That's great. I know you don't have much time, but I think it's important for you to see him regularly. He's been depressed recently.

lines.speaker: female
lines.text: Yeah, I know. He still complained about being there, though.

lines.speaker: male
lines.text: Well, that's understandable. He used to be very independent.

lines.speaker: female
lines.text: Look, do you think we could go again this weekend?

lines.speaker: male
lines.text: I'll have to check my work schedule, but that should be fine.

questions.question: What does the man say?
choices: He does not enjoy visiting his wife's father.
He should have gone with his wife.
The nursing home is too far away.
His wife should spend time with her father.
questions.explanation: 男性は妻が定期的に父親に会うことが大切だと思っており、週末にまた行くことにも前向きです。
highlights: I think it's important for you to see him regularly
questions.choiceAnalysis: 選択肢1「訪問が嫌」→一緒に行くと言っている。選択肢2「一緒に行くべきだった」→次は行く。選択肢3「遠すぎる」→言及なし。選択肢4「父親と時間を過ごすべき」→正解。

lines.speaker: male
lines.text: That's the worst round of golf I've played in months.

lines.speaker: female
lines.text: You didn't do that badly.

lines.speaker: male
lines.text: You're too nice. But I really struggled. My putting was terrible.

lines.speaker: female
lines.text: Well, the wind was pretty strong.

lines.speaker: male
lines.text: I'd like to blame my old clubs, but I feel like something is wrong with my form.

lines.speaker: female
lines.text: Working with an instructor might not be a bad idea.

lines.speaker: male
lines.text: Maybe you're right. I seem to have developed some bad habits.

lines.speaker: female
lines.text: The professionals here at the club are reasonably priced.

lines.speaker: male
lines.text: Really? I'll look into it.

questions.question: What does the woman suggest the man do?
choices: Play golf with her more often.
Avoid playing golf on windy days.
Buy some new golf clubs.
Take golf lessons.
questions.explanation: 女性はインストラクターと練習することを提案しています。
highlights: Working with an instructor might not be a bad idea
questions.choiceAnalysis: 選択肢1「一緒にゴルフ」→言及なし。選択肢2「風の日を避ける」→言及なし。選択肢3「新しいクラブ」→フォームの問題。選択肢4「ゴルフレッスン」→正解。

lines.speaker: female
lines.text: Hey Antonio! What are you doing here? I thought you'd found a job overseas.

lines.speaker: male
lines.text: I did, but I'm home visiting my parents.

lines.speaker: female
lines.text: They must be happy to see you. How long are you here for?

lines.speaker: male
lines.text: I go back to Turkey in a couple of days. Listen, I've got a few hours to kill before going to a concert. Do you want to get some lunch?

lines.speaker: female
lines.text: I'd love to, if you don't mind me running a few quick errands first.

lines.speaker: male
lines.text: Not a problem. I'll come along and keep you company.

lines.speaker: female
lines.text: Great. We have so much to catch up on.

questions.question: What is the man going to do next?
choices: Visit his parents.
Wait at a restaurant.
Join the woman on her errands.
Get some tickets for the concert.
questions.explanation: 男性は女性の用事に付き合うと言っています。
highlights: I'll come along and keep you company
questions.choiceAnalysis: 選択肢1「両親を訪ねる」→既に訪問中。選択肢2「レストランで待つ」→一緒に行く。選択肢3「女性の用事に付き合う」→正解。選択肢4「チケットを買う」→コンサートは後で。

parts.name: 第2部
parts.description: 英文を聞き、その質問に対して最も適切な答えを1, 2, 3, 4の中から一つ選ぶ形式です。

lines.speaker: narrator
lines.text: Much of the world's gold was formed deep beneath the ground, but it's not always necessary to dig deep mines to find this valuable metal. A technique called placer mining is used to find gold in or near rivers. In this type of mining, machines are often used to collect water, sand, and small stones in huge containers. The materials are then shaken and the lighter substances fall out. Because gold is heavy, it will remain in the bottom of the containers.

questions.question: What technique does placer mining rely on?
choices: Building dams to block rivers.
Digging deep beneath the ground.
Drilling holes in rocks.
Separating gold from lighter materials.
questions.explanation: 砂金採掘は容器を振って軽い物質を落とし、重い金を底に残す技術です。
highlights: The materials are then shaken and the lighter substances fall out. Because gold is heavy, it will remain
questions.choiceAnalysis: 選択肢1「ダム」→言及なし。選択肢2「深く掘る」→深く掘る必要がない。選択肢3「岩に穴」→言及なし。選択肢4「軽い物質から金を分離」→正解。

lines.speaker: narrator
lines.text: Some spots are better for placer mining than others. For example, gold is slowly washed downriver, but because it is heavy, it tends to build up where there is a sudden drop, such as at waterfalls. The falling water creates pools at the bottom of the waterfall that can trap significant amounts of gold. These are the areas that are of most interest to mining companies.

questions.question: Where are the best areas to find gold when placer mining?
choices: Where the level of a river suddenly changes.
Where the current of a river is strongest.
Where a river suddenly becomes narrower.
Where a river is shallow and wide.
questions.explanation: 滝のように急に落差がある場所に金が溜まりやすいです。
highlights: it tends to build up where there is a sudden drop, such as at waterfalls
questions.choiceAnalysis: 選択肢1「川の水位が急に変わる場所」→正解。選択肢2「流れが最も強い」→言及なし。選択肢3「川が狭くなる」→言及なし。選択肢4「浅くて広い」→言及なし。

lines.speaker: narrator
lines.text: A recent study by researchers has revealed new information about the eating habits of Neanderthals. Using DNA analysis techniques, the researchers compared substances found on the teeth of Neanderthals that lived in northern Spain with substances found on the teeth of Neanderthals in Belgium. The results showed that the Neanderthals in Belgium consumed mainly wild sheep and other mammals, while the Neanderthals in Spain had a plant-based diet.

questions.question: How were the Neanderthals in Belgium different from those in Spain?
choices: The Belgian Neanderthals were more intelligent.
The Belgian Neanderthals ate mostly meat.
The Spanish Neanderthals tended to live longer.
The Spanish Neanderthals were better at fighting.
questions.explanation: ベルギーのネアンデルタール人は主に野生の羊などの哺乳類（肉）を食べ、スペインは植物中心でした。
highlights: the Neanderthals in Belgium consumed mainly wild sheep and other mammals
questions.choiceAnalysis: 選択肢1「知能が高い」→言及なし。選択肢2「主に肉を食べた」→正解。選択肢3「スペインが長寿」→言及なし。選択肢4「戦闘能力」→言及なし。

lines.speaker: narrator
lines.text: The researchers also found evidence that a Neanderthal in Spain was suffering from a stomach illness and an infected tooth. DNA from pain-relieving plants was found between his teeth. The researchers believe this indicates that Neanderthals knew that such plants could be used to help reduce pain. If true, it seems that Neanderthals were both more sophisticated and better able to take advantage of their environment than previously thought.

questions.question: What is one thing the researchers believe about the Neanderthals in Spain?
choices: They were very few in number.
They caught few diseases.
They treated some of their own health problems.
They rarely suffered from stomach problems.
questions.explanation: 痛みを和らげる植物のDNAが歯から発見され、自分で健康問題に対処していたと考えられています。
highlights: Neanderthals knew that such plants could be used to help reduce pain
questions.choiceAnalysis: 選択肢1「少数」→言及なし。選択肢2「病気にかからない」→胃の病気にかかっていた。選択肢3「自分で健康問題に対処」→正解。選択肢4「胃の問題が少ない」→胃の病気があった。

lines.speaker: narrator
lines.text: Elisabeth Amalie Eugenie, commonly known as "Sisi," became the empress of Austria when she married the country's emperor in 1854. However, Sisi had little interest in the duties of royalty, such as ceremonies and policy discussions. She began traveling to escape court life, but this angered her powerful mother-in-law, who considered Sisi's behavior inappropriate for an empress.

questions.question: What is one thing we learn about Sisi?
choices: She rarely disagreed with her husband.
She had always wanted to become empress.
She tried to avoid some of her royal duties.
She relied on support from her mother-in-law.
questions.explanation: シシは王室の義務にほとんど興味がなく、宮廷生活から逃れるために旅に出ました。
highlights: Sisi had little interest in the duties of royalty
She began traveling to escape court life
questions.choiceAnalysis: 選択肢1「夫と対立しない」→言及なし。選択肢2「皇后になりたかった」→興味がなかった。選択肢3「王室の義務を避けた」→正解。選択肢4「義母に頼った」→義母は怒った。

lines.speaker: narrator
lines.text: On her travels, Sisi developed a strong relationship with the people of Hungary. At the time, Hungary was ruled by Austria, and Sisi felt that the Hungarians were treated unfairly. When she was back in Austria, she campaigned for Hungarians to have more legal rights and helped them achieve some level of independence from Austria. Sisi was also interested in mental health, and she campaigned for improved care for mentally ill patients. She even asked for the construction of a hospital as a present from her husband.

questions.question: What was one way Sisi helped the Hungarian people?
choices: She volunteered at a hospital in Hungary.
She donated all her wealth to them.
She helped to improve their legal situation.
She made it easier for them to enter Austria.
questions.explanation: シシはハンガリー人がより多くの法的権利を持てるよう運動しました。
highlights: she campaigned for Hungarians to have more legal rights
questions.choiceAnalysis: 選択肢1「病院でボランティア」→言及なし。選択肢2「財産を寄付」→言及なし。選択肢3「法的状況を改善」→正解。選択肢4「オーストリア入国を容易に」→言及なし。

lines.speaker: narrator
lines.text: In recent years, standing meetings have become more popular at many companies. To study the effects of such meetings, researchers at the University of Missouri conducted an experiment in which participants did group problem-solving exercises while either standing or sitting down. The researchers had predicted that the standing groups would rush and make poor decisions. Although the standing groups did take less time to complete their tasks, their decisions were mostly the same as those of the sitting groups.

questions.question: What did the University of Missouri study reveal?
choices: Standing meetings took longer than sit-down meetings.
Standing meetings had little impact on decisions.
Participants preferred sit-down meetings.
Sit-down meetings helped people to be creative.
questions.explanation: 立ってミーティングをしても意思決定の質はほとんど変わりませんでした。
highlights: their decisions were mostly the same as those of the sitting groups
questions.choiceAnalysis: 選択肢1「立って会議の方が長い」→短かった。選択肢2「意思決定にほとんど影響なし」→正解。選択肢3「座る方が好まれた」→言及なし。選択肢4「座ると創造的」→言及なし。

lines.speaker: narrator
lines.text: Standing meetings have their critics, however. Business consultant Bob Frisch argues that when people are standing, the physical differences between them become more important. This can lead to shorter people feeling nervous and therefore being less likely to share their thoughts. Additionally, young people's greater physical stamina could give them an advantage if a negotiation during a standing meeting becomes lengthy.

questions.question: What is one criticism Bob Frisch has of standing meetings?
choices: They may be unfair to some participants.
They can encourage people to be too casual.
They may harm young people's physical health.
They can lead to lengthier negotiations.
questions.explanation: 背の低い人が緊張して意見を言いにくくなるなど、参加者間の公平性に問題があると指摘しています。
highlights: shorter people feeling nervous and therefore being less likely to share their thoughts
questions.choiceAnalysis: 選択肢1「一部の参加者に不公平」→正解。選択肢2「カジュアルになりすぎる」→言及なし。選択肢3「健康に害」→言及なし。選択肢4「交渉が長引く」→若者に有利になるだけ。

lines.speaker: narrator
lines.text: When most people think about the settling of the American West, they imagine people crossing the country in wagons pulled by horses. However, the mule, an animal created by breeding a female horse with a male donkey, was preferred by pioneers. Mules are strong but weigh less and eat less than horses. They also suffer fewer leg injuries, and they can see things to the side more easily. Pioneers found that these characteristics made mules more suitable for traveling over ground that was steep, uneven, or rocky.

questions.question: What is one thing we learn about mules?
choices: They eat more than horses.
They can move over difficult ground.
They cannot pull heavy wagons.
They have relatively poor eyesight.
questions.explanation: ラバは急な、平らでない、岩の多い地形の移動に適していました。
highlights: mules more suitable for traveling over ground that was steep, uneven, or rocky
questions.choiceAnalysis: 選択肢1「馬より食べる」→食べない。選択肢2「困難な地形を移動できる」→正解。選択肢3「重い荷馬車を引けない」→引けた。選択肢4「視力が悪い」→側方視が良い。

lines.speaker: narrator
lines.text: One disadvantage, however, was that, unlike horses, which follow almost any command given to them, mules generally do what they want. For example, wagon drivers often experienced great difficulties in getting mules to cross rivers that the animals thought might be dangerous. Despite this disadvantage, westward journeys would have been nearly impossible without them.

questions.question: Why was using mules problematic?
choices: They sometimes attacked wagon drivers.
They often suffered leg injuries.
They required large amounts of water.
They sometimes refused to obey orders.
questions.explanation: ラバは自分のやりたいことをするため、危険だと思う川を渡らせるのが困難でした。
highlights: mules generally do what they want
great difficulties in getting mules to cross rivers
questions.choiceAnalysis: 選択肢1「御者を攻撃」→言及なし。選択肢2「脚をケガ」→ケガが少ない。選択肢3「大量の水」→言及なし。選択肢4「命令に従わない」→正解。

lines.speaker: narrator
lines.text: Steve Jobs, the founder of Apple Inc., had a role model named Edwin Land. Land founded a camera company called Polaroid that was well-known when Jobs was growing up. Jobs agreed with many of Land's ideas. For example, Jobs often said, "Customers don't know what they want until we've shown them." This was a concept he learned from Land. Both Jobs and Land thought that conducting market research on consumer preferences was pointless. They believed companies should make totally new and interesting products their customers had never dreamed of.

questions.question: What was one belief held by Steve Jobs and Edwin Land?
choices: Customers want products that last.
Market research is unnecessary.
Advertising should explain concepts simply.
Products should be made from recycled materials.
questions.explanation: 二人とも消費者調査は無意味だと考え、顧客が夢にも思わない全く新しい製品を作るべきだと信じていました。
highlights: conducting market research on consumer preferences was pointless
questions.choiceAnalysis: 選択肢1「長持ちする製品」→言及なし。選択肢2「市場調査は不要」→正解。選択肢3「広告をシンプルに」→言及なし。選択肢4「リサイクル素材」→言及なし。

lines.speaker: narrator
lines.text: Both Jobs and Land were also famous for trying to make even minor design details of their products perfect. Land, for example, spent so much money on product development that financial experts warned against investing in Polaroid's stock. However, his products were thought to be beautifully designed and his company became highly profitable.

questions.question: What were both Jobs and Land famous for?
choices: Focusing on design details.
Listening to advice from financial experts.
Keeping investors happy.
Sharing technology with each other.
questions.explanation: 二人は製品の細かいデザインまで完璧にすることで有名でした。
highlights: famous for trying to make even minor design details of their products perfect
questions.choiceAnalysis: 選択肢1「デザインの細部にこだわる」→正解。選択肢2「専門家の助言を聞く」→無視した。選択肢3「投資家を喜ばせる」→投資を心配させた。選択肢4「技術共有」→言及なし。

parts.name: 第3部
parts.description: 現実の場面を想定したリスニング問題です。音声を聞き、最も適切な答えを選んでください。

questions.situation: You teach part-time at Sunset English School, but you are not available on Thursdays or Fridays. The school manager leaves you a voice mail.

lines.speaker: narrator
lines.text: Hi, it's Sylvester from Sunset. I'm calling about the advanced grammar course you're scheduled to teach on Mondays from next month. Unfortunately, only three students have signed up, and our minimum class size is five. So we have a couple of options. We could reschedule the advanced class for Thursdays, since two more students could attend then. The other option is to make your class an intermediate one. Most of the students available on Mondays are intermediate students. Finding another teacher at this point is not possible, so we appreciate your cooperation.

questions.question: What should you do?
choices: Teach the advanced class on another day.
Wait a month before beginning the advanced class.
Ask the school to find another teacher.
Teach an intermediate class on Mondays.
questions.explanation: 木曜・金曜は空いていない→木曜の上級クラスは不可。他の教師も見つからない。月曜に中級クラスを教えるのが唯一の選択肢。
highlights: The other option is to make your class an intermediate one
questions.choiceAnalysis: 選択肢1「別の日に上級」→木曜は不可。選択肢2「1ヶ月待つ」→提案されていない。選択肢3「他の教師を探す」→不可能。選択肢4「月曜に中級」→正解。

questions.situation: You call about a newspaper subscription. You want to read the daily news online and get printed copies of just the weekend edition.

lines.speaker: narrator
lines.text: We offer several subscription options. The Net Special Plan provides unlimited access to daily news on our website for just $10 a month. Our Premium Plan adds access to online daily puzzles and leisure magazines. That's just $14 a month. Next, the Informed Plan includes the Net Special Plan and home delivery of the printed weekend edition for $20 a month. Finally, if you prefer reading all your news on paper, choose our Comprehensive Plan. You'll get delivery of our newspapers every day, plus unlimited access to our digital content for $30 a month.

questions.question: Which plan should you choose?
choices: The Net Special Plan.
The Premium Plan.
The Informed Plan.
The Comprehensive Plan.
questions.explanation: オンラインで日刊ニュース＋週末版の印刷配達→Informed Plan（Net Special＋週末版印刷配達）が条件にぴったり。
highlights: the Informed Plan includes the Net Special Plan and home delivery of the printed weekend edition
questions.choiceAnalysis: 選択肢1「Net Special」→オンラインのみ、印刷なし。選択肢2「Premium」→パズルと雑誌、印刷なし。選択肢3「Informed」→正解。選択肢4「Comprehensive」→毎日配達で過剰。

questions.situation: You attend an orientation for first-year students at a UK university. You will study for a biology degree, and you already have a student ID card.

lines.speaker: narrator
lines.text: OK, we'll now divide you into groups to receive academic guidance from the teaching staff in your specific department. Those in humanities and language departments should head to Vernal Hall. That's near the main building where you first checked in. Those studying for science degrees should go to Baxter Auditorium, located directly across from the entrance to this building. Engineering students can stay in this hall. Students who have still not received their student ID card should go back to the main building after their academic guidance meeting. You all have maps in your orientation packet if you need to check locations.

questions.question: What should you do now?
choices: Remain where you are.
Go to Vernal Hall.
Go to Baxter Auditorium.
Return to the main building.
questions.explanation: 生物学は理学部→Baxter Auditoriumに行く。学生証は既に持っている。
highlights: Those studying for science degrees should go to Baxter Auditorium
questions.choiceAnalysis: 選択肢1「そのまま」→工学部向け。選択肢2「Vernal Hall」→人文・語学。選択肢3「Baxter Auditorium」→正解。理学部。選択肢4「本館」→学生証未取得者向け。

questions.situation: You are at a bakery choosing a dessert for a dinner party you will host at home. You are allergic to dairy products, and one guest cannot eat nuts.

lines.speaker: narrator
lines.text: Let me tell you about our seasonal special. It's Peach Paradise Pie, which uses fresh peaches and authentic dairy custard—it's very creamy and absolutely heavenly! On the regular menu, the Stars-and-Stripes Apple Cake is a popular dessert this time of year, and it contains no dairy or nuts. Our biggest seller this month is Turtle Clusters. They're packed with caramel, hazelnuts, and pecan nuts. Last but not least, we have the Carrot Cake Delight, which has walnuts and raisins. It uses locally made butter, and our customers love it.

questions.question: Which dessert should you choose?
choices: The Peach Paradise Pie.
The Stars-and-Stripes Apple Cake.
The Turtle Clusters.
The Carrot Cake Delight.
questions.explanation: 乳製品アレルギー＋ゲストがナッツを食べられない。Peach Pie→乳製品。Turtle Clusters→ナッツ。Carrot Cake→バター（乳製品）とナッツ。Apple Cake→乳製品もナッツも含まない。
highlights: the Stars-and-Stripes Apple Cake is a popular dessert this time of year, and it contains no dairy or nuts
questions.choiceAnalysis: 選択肢1「Peach Pie」→乳製品カスタード。選択肢2「Apple Cake」→正解。乳製品・ナッツなし。選択肢3「Turtle Clusters」→ナッツ入り。選択肢4「Carrot Cake」→バターとナッツ。

questions.situation: You are an engineer. You want to change jobs but cannot accept a lower salary. A recruitment consultant tells you the following.

lines.speaker: narrator
lines.text: There's an opening at MasterTech. The job is similar to what you do now. It's a successful, well-run business, but they cannot match your current pay. Next up, Blau Industrial is looking for a senior engineer. Your experience definitely qualifies you, and you'd get a pay raise, too. Finally, QCS Systems has an opening. The salary at QCS can vary a lot based on bonuses. It's likely you'll earn less than you do now some years. I thought there'd be a job coming up with Redwin, but they've decided not to replace the engineer who's leaving.

questions.question: Which company should you apply to?
choices: MasterTech.
Blau Industrial.
QCS Systems.
Redwin.
questions.explanation: 給料が下がらないことが条件。MasterTech→現在の給料に合わない。QCS→年によって給料が下がる可能性。Redwin→募集なし。Blau Industrial→昇給あり。
highlights: Blau Industrial is looking for a senior engineer
you'd get a pay raise, too
questions.choiceAnalysis: 選択肢1「MasterTech」→給料が下がる。選択肢2「Blau Industrial」→正解。昇給。選択肢3「QCS」→給料が変動。選択肢4「Redwin」→募集なし。
